title: Private schools can hike fees without prior government approval if declared before academic session: Delhi HC
description: The Delhi High Court has held that private unaided recognised schools in Delhi can increase their fees at the beginning of an academic session without prior approval from the Directorate of Education (DoE), provided the proposed fee structure is declared before the academic session begins [DPS Vs Government of Delhi].Justice Anup Jairam Bhambhani ruled that Section 17(3) of the Delhi School Education Act, 1973 does not require such schools to wait for the DoE’s approval before implementing a fee
